era1_seed.narrative: Kai scaffolding was the ONLY activity. ICM got minor README updates but the developer was laser-focused on the Atelier/Liminal task chain. No other repos active.

themes: Built from scratch in one day
hydra-creative-agent.note: This repo was MERGED into Liminal the same day — feat: Merge Hydra intelligence layer into Liminal (Phases 2-6)

atelier.note: Atelier was Liminal before the rename — these commits ARE Liminal history

era2_explosion.narrative: THE EXPLOSION was not just Liminal. The developer built an entire creative AI system (hydra-creative-agent) from scratch with 46 commits in ONE DAY, then merged it into Liminal. Simultaneously doing a 34-item audit on the atelier codebase. This was the most parallel activity of the entire period.

era3_consolidation.narrative: Pure Liminal day. Zero other repo activity. The developer was entirely focused on consolidating the merge — swarm system, Compost Mill, prompt overhaul, provider removal.

mcp-video.note: MASSIVE concurrent project. mcp-video went from nothing to v0.5.0 with 55 commits while Liminal got 69 commits. Developer was working both simultaneously.

DialectOS.note: Born on Mar 22 during Liminal's LIR/chat push

era4-5_quality_conversational.narrative: The Quality Crusade and Conversational Turn were CONCURRENT with the birth of mcp-video (55 commits!) and DialectOS (8 commits). The developer was managing 4-5 active projects simultaneously. Mar 22 alone saw 45 Liminal commits + 28 mcp-video commits + 8 DialectOS commits = 81 total commits across repos.

era6_quiet.narrative: THE QUIET was not a break. The developer built the ENTIRE PuenteWorks website (14 commits over 2 days), shipped mcp-video v0.6-v0.8, launched site-to-stitch, and updated their GitHub profile. The 'quiet' was a Liminal break, not a productivity break — the developer was building infrastructure and business assets.

mcp-video.note: mcp-video hit v1.0 and went through 6 version bumps to v1.2 during Liminal's multimedia push

cerafica.note: Cerafica (ceramics e-commerce) got massive attention — 11 commits on Mar 28 alone

era7-8_multimedia_dogfood.narrative: The Multimedia + Dogfood era was shared with mcp-video reaching v1.0 and Cerafica launching its e-commerce site. The developer was simultaneously building video infrastructure in THREE places: Liminal (Remotion integration), mcp-video (v1.0 release), and Cerafica (product videos). Mar 29 was the GitHub Guardian audit day — 9 repos got baseline security audits.

quiet_period_activity.interpretation: The Quiet was a strategic pause from Liminal to ship external projects. PuenteWorks (business website) was the primary focus, followed by mcp-video reaching Remotion integration. The developer was NOT resting — they were building the business infrastructure around their creative tools.

concurrent_repos.relationship: SISTER PROJECT — video generation MCP server. Born same day as Liminal's Quality Crusade. Shared Remotion infrastructure with Liminal's video pipeline.
PRE-LIMINAL — Atelier WAS Liminal before the rename. All commits from Feb 28-Mar 19 are shared history.
ABSORBED INTO LIMINAL — Built from scratch on Mar 19, merged into Liminal the same day. The 'Hydra intelligence layer' became Liminal's core.
PARALLEL — Spanish translation MCP server. Published to npm. Active during Liminal quiet and Bible days.
PRECEDING — Ceramic glaze management. Built in the 4 days BEFORE the Mar 19 Explosion. May have been a creative warm-up.
PARALLEL — Ceramics e-commerce site. Product photos, Pages deployment, Instagram pipeline. Business side of the ceramics creative practice.
QUIET PERIOD — The developer's business/consulting website. Built during the 4-day Liminal silence.
PERIPHERAL — Cross-workspace research and agent architecture. Evolved into Hermes profiles and founder personality analysis.
QUIET PERIOD — Multi-agent strategic decision system. Born Mar 22, expanded Mar 24 during Liminal silence.
SUPPORTING — Developer's portfolio site, restyled to match Cerafica theme.
PRECEDING — Methodology framework. Active Feb 22-Mar 14, went COMPLETELY SILENT once Liminal's Explosion hit on Mar 19. The methodology work may have been preparation for the build sprint.
PRECEDING — Research pipeline with human-door integration. Went silent before Liminal started.
PRECEDING — Open-source ceramic glaze SaaS. Born Mar 16, just before the Explosion.
NEW — Born on Bible day. Opportunity pipeline for Paperclip.
QUIET PERIOD — Born during the Liminal silence.
QUIET PERIOD — Profile update during the silence.

early_march.narrative: The 2 weeks before Liminal's Explosion were dominated by ICM methodology work and research pipeline infrastructure. This looks like a preparatory phase — establishing the methodological framework before the creative coding sprint.

mar_15-18.narrative: The 4 days immediately BEFORE the Explosion were a ceramics deep-dive. 38 GlazeLab commits, 17 reverse-engineering commits. The developer was building glaze infrastructure that may have primed the creative energy for the Mar 19 detonation.

insights: 1. THE QUIET WAS NOT A BREAK. During the 4-day Liminal silence (Mar 24-27), the developer shipped 25 commits across 6 repos — building PuenteWorks from scratch (14 commits), shipping mcp-video v0.6→v0.8, launching site-to-stitch, and updating their GitHub profile. They were building business infrastructure, not resting.

2. PRE-LIMINAL CERAMICS WARM-UP. The 4 days before the Explosion (Mar 15-18) saw 56 commits across ceramics repos (GlazeLab, reverse-engineering, openglaze). This ceramics deep-dive may have been the creative warm-up that primed the Mar 19 detonation.

3. THE EXPLOSION WAS THREE-HEADED. On Mar 19, the developer was simultaneously: (a) renaming Atelier to Liminal and merging code (23 commits), (b) building hydra-creative-agent from scratch (46 commits), and (c) auditing the atelier codebase (18 commits). Total: 87 commits across 3 repos in one day. The hydra system was absorbed into Liminal the same day it was created.

4. MCP-VIDEO WAS LIMINAL'S SISTER PROJECT. Born on Mar 21 during Liminal's Quality Crusade, mcp-video received 55 commits while Liminal got 69 in the same window. Both converged on Remotion for video generation — shared infrastructure.

5. MAR 22 WAS THE BUSIEST DAY. 81 total commits across repos: 45 Liminal + 28 mcp-video + 8 DialectOS. This was the day LIR, Chat CLI, and compost dual-model were built in Liminal, while mcp-video shipped FFmpeg Waves 1-5 and DialectOS was born.

6. ICM METHODOLOGY WENT SILENT ON MAR 19. Interpreted-Context-Methdology was active from Feb 22 through Mar 14 (32 commits), then completely stopped when Liminal's Explosion hit. The methodology work may have been laying the intellectual groundwork for the creative coding sprint.

7. THE MAR 29 GUARDIAN AUDIT WAS A COORDINATED SWEEP. On Mar 29, 9 repos simultaneously received 'GitHub Guardian baseline audit' commits. This was a systematic security/documentation review across the entire portfolio, not isolated incidents.

8. PUENTEWEEKS WAS A BUSINESS PIVOT. The developer built their entire consulting/business website (14 commits in 2 days) during the Liminal quiet period. This suggests the 'quiet' was actually strategic — they needed to establish the business side before continuing the creative tool.

9. REPOS THAT FED INTO LIMINAL: atelier (direct rename), hydra-creative-agent (absorbed same day), Interpreted-Context-Methdology (methodological foundation). The developer didn't just build Liminal — they merged three separate projects into it.

10. CERAMICS AS CREATIVE PRACTICE. GlazeLab, reverse-engineering, openglaze, and cerafica collectively show the developer running a real ceramics practice (glaze chemistry, RE pipeline, e-commerce) parallel to the software work. Cerafica product photos and Instagram pipelines suggest active pottery sales.

11. THE BIBLE DAY HAD THREE THREADS. Apr 1 saw 53 Liminal commits (THE BIBLE), 12 DialectOS commits (translation hardening), and 9 reverse-engineering commits (Hermes agent architecture). The developer was simultaneously documenting Liminal's theology, hardening a translation system, and building personality profiles.

12. BURST PATTERN, NOT SUSTAINED. The developer works in extreme bursts: 87 commits on Mar 19, 81 effective commits on Mar 22, 53 on Apr 1. Between bursts, they switch domains entirely rather than resting.
